description: Lock aluminum_smelting to industrial primary aluminum smelting operations built around the canonical Hall-Heroult process, avoiding duplicate low-level edges.

checks.reason: The node is scoped to industrial primary smelting operations, anchored to Pittsburgh Reduction Company's 1888 pilot production.
Primary aluminum smelting at this scope requires the Hall-Heroult electrolytic reduction process.
Electricity is represented through the Hall-Heroult process node, not duplicated as a direct smelting-operation edge.
The electrochemistry relationship is represented through the Hall-Heroult process node.
Medieval metallurgy is too broad and indirect for the scoped industrial primary aluminum smelting operation.
